name: build123d (Modern Code-First CAD)

performance.notes: OpenCASCADE via OCP; similar performance to CadQuery

agent_readiness.notes: Modern successor to CadQuery using context managers for cleaner code. from build123d import *. with BuildPart() as box: Box(10, 10, 5); Hole(3). with BuildSketch() as sketch: Circle(5); Rectangle(8, 8, mode=Mode.SUBTRACT). extrude(sketch.sketch, 10). 3 builders: BuildPart (3D), BuildSketch (2D), BuildLine (1D). Selectors: box.faces().sort_by(Axis.Z)[-1]. Topology: Compound, Solid, Shell, Face, Edge, Vertex. Export: STEP, STL, SVG, DXF. Jupyter: show_object(). Python type hints throughout. Newer than CadQuery with more Pythonic API.

gotchas: Younger project than CadQuery — fewer examples and community resources
Context manager pattern differs from CadQuery's fluent chain — migration not trivial
Still pre-1.0 — some API breakage between minor versions
best_when: New parametric CAD projects where you prefer context managers over fluent chains
When type hints and IDE autocompletion matter
avoid_when: When stable, mature ecosystem is required (use CadQuery 2.x)
summary: build123d is a modern code-first 3D CAD library using context managers. with BuildPart() as p: Box(10,10,5); Hole(3). BuildSketch for 2D profiles, BuildLine for wires. Solid topology API, full type hints. STEP/STL/SVG export. Jupyter show_object(). Apache-2.0 by gumyr. More Pythonic than CadQuery but younger. Pre-1.0 — minor version API changes. Best for new projects preferring context-manager style CAD.
